title: Find and fix a case-sensitivity bug buried in a many-file router
task: A small URL router in src/ resolves request paths against route patterns. Static route segments are supposed to be CASE-SENSITIVE, but `/users/42` is wrongly matching the pattern `/Users/:id`. Two tests in test/router.test.js fail. Find the responsible module among the many helpers in src/ and fix it so the whole suite passes. Do NOT modify test/router.test.js, and do NOT weaken any other behavior (param capture, query stripping, decoding).
notes: Long-context navigation: 18 modules in src/, only matchStatic.js is wrong (it lower-cases both sides). The symptom shows up via resolve()/matchRoute() in index.js/router.js, several hops from the root cause. The file_contains/file_not_contains on matchStatic.js confirm the fix is in the right place, not papered over upstream.
